metadata.title: BC Building Code Amendments to NBC 2020 - Part 9
metadata.description: British Columbia-specific amendments for Part 9 (Housing and Small Buildings)
metadata.author: BC Building Code Team

amendment.description: Add note reference to Article 9.36.1.1.(1)
Add new sentence (9) defining log homes in Article 9.36.1.2

sentence.text: For the purposes of this Section, the term "log homes" shall mean homes with a type of construction whereby the exterior vertical opaque walls primarily consist of structural log members.

amendment.description: Reorder clauses in alphabetical order in Article 9.37.1.3.(1)

sentence.text: Except as provided in to , buildings shall comply with

clause.text: the prescriptive or trade-off requirements in to ,
the performance requirements in ,
the tiered performance requirements in ,
the tiered prescriptive requirements in , or
the .

amendment.description: Replace sentence content with BC-specific compliance requirements in Article 9.36.1.3.(3)

sentence.text: Except as permitted in , buildings described in and shall comply with :

clause.text: houses with or without a secondary suite including their common spaces, and

except for common spaces in a house with a secondary suite, buildings containing only dwelling units and common spaces whose total floor area does not exceed 20% of the total floor area of the building.

amendment.description: Replace sentence 6 and add sentences 7-8 with BC-specific requirements in Article 9.37.1.3.(6)-(8)

sentence.text: Notwithstanding and , and except for the purpose of modeling the reference building for compliance with and , Tables 9.37.2.6.-C, 9.37.2.7.-D, and 9.37.2.8.-C apply only to

clause.text: houses with or without a secondary suite including their common spaces, and

except for common spaces in a house with its secondary suite, buildings containing only dwelling units and common spaces whose total floor area does not exceed 20% of the total floor area of the building.

sentence.text: Log homes shall conform to the requirements of

clause.text: to ,
, or

sentence.text: Notwithstanding , Tables 9.37.2.6.-C, 9.37.2.7.-D, and 9.37.2.8.-C do not apply to log homes.

amendment.description: Add clause (c) with Table 9.37.2.6.-C requirements and note reference in Article 9.37.2.6.(1)

sentence.text: Except as provided in and and and , the effective thermal resistance of above-ground opaque building assemblies or portions thereof shall be not less than that shown for the applicable heating-degree day category in

clause.text: , where the ventilation system does not include heat-recovery equipment, or
, where the ventilation system includes heat-recovery equipment conforming to , or
Table 9.37.2.6.-C, where the ventilation or space conditioning system contain either:

subclause.text: a heat-recovery ventilator conforming to , or
a heat pump in conformance with .

sentence.text: (See .)

amendment.description: Insert new Table 9.37.2.6.-C for buildings containing only dwelling units with separate notes section

table.title: Effective Thermal Resistance of Above-ground Opaque Assemblies for Buildings Containing Only Dwelling Units

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,(1) in Celsius Degree-Days

note.text: Notes to Table 9.37.2.6.-C:
(1) See .
(2) Notwithstanding , ceilings below attics shall not have a reduction in effective thermal resistance
(3) For the purposes of this table, a roof deck shall mean a horizontal portion of a roof intended for occupancy.
(4) For the purposes of this table, flat roofs shall mean a roof that is not intended for occupancy.
(5) See for requirements concerning the above-ground portion of foundation walls.

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.37.2.7 with BC version containing 11 sentences and new Table D

article.title: Thermal Characteristics of Fenestration, Doors and Skylights

sentence.text: Except as provided in to and , fenestration and doors shall have an overall thermal transmittance (U-value) not greater than the values listed in for the applicable heating-degree day category. (See )

table.title: Required Thermal Characteristics of Fenestration and Doors

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,See . in Celsius Degree-Days
FenestrationExcept skylights and glass block assemblies. and doors

sentence.text: Skylights shall have an overall thermal transmittance not greater than the values listed in for the applicable heating-degree day category. (See )

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,See . in Celsius Degree-Days

sentence.text: Except for site-assembled or site-glazed factory-made fenestration products, curtain wall construction, and site-built windows and glazed doors that are tested in accordance with , site-built windows and glazed doors need not comply with , provided they are constructed in accordance with one of the options presented in for the applicable climate zone. (See )

table.title: Compliance Options for Site-built Windows and Glazed Portion of Doors

sentence.text: Glass block assemblies separating conditioned space from unconditioned space or the exterior shall have

clause.text: an overall thermal transmittance of not more than 2.9 W/(m2×K), and
a total aggregate area of not more than 1.85 m2.

sentence.text: One door separating a conditioned space from an unconditioned space or the exterior is permitted to have an overall thermal transmittance up to 2.6 W/(m2×K).
Storm windows and doors need not comply with .
Vehicular access doors separating a conditioned space from an unconditioned space or the exterior shall have a nominal thermal resistance of not less than 1.1 (m2×K)/W.
Access hatches separating a conditioned space from an unconditioned space shall be insulated to a nominal thermal resistance of not less than 2.6 (m2×K)/W.
A door separating a conditioned space from an unconditional space or the exterior is not required to conform to if,

clause.text: in the case of a building in a location with a heating degree-day value of less than or equal to 3999, the door is one of not more than three nonconforming doors, each of which has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.10 W/m2·K,

in the case of a building in a location with a heating degree-day value of at least 4000 and not greater than 5999, the door is one of not more than two nonconforming doors, each of which has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.10 W/m2·K,

in the case of a building in a location with a heating degree-day value of greater than or equal to 6000, the door is one of not more than two nonconforming doors, each of which has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.00 W/m2·K, or
in any case, the door is the only nonconforming door and has an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.60 W/m2·K.

sentence.text: A building described in or is permitted to have an additional nonconforming door with an overall thermal transmittance not greater than 2.10 W/m2·K if the effective thermal resistance of the ceilings of the building is at least 0.88 m2·K/W greater than the relevant value shown in or , as applicable.
For buildings described in , fenestration shall have an overall thermal transmittance (U-value) not greater than the values listed in for the applicable heating-degree day category.

table.title: Required Thermal Characteristics of Fenestration for Buildings Containing Only Dwelling Units

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,See . in Celsius Degree-Days
FenestrationExcept doors, skylights and glass block assemblies.

table-notes.note: Except doors (see and ), skylights (see and ) and glass block assemblies (see ).

amendment.description: Add clause c) referencing Table 9.36.2.8.-C for heat pump and HRV systems in Article 9.36.2.8.(1)

clause.text: , where the ventilation or space conditioning systems contain either:

subclause.text: a heat-recovery ventilator conforming to , or
a heat pump in conformance with .

amendment.description: Insert Table 9.36.2.8.-C for buildings with heat pumps or HRVs

table.title: Effective Thermal Resistance of Assemblies Below-Grade or in Contact with the Ground for Buildings Containing Only Dwelling Units

row.entry: Building Assembly Below-Grade or in Contact with the Ground(1)
Heating Degree-Days of Building Location,(2) in Celsius Degree-Days
Heated and unheated floors on permafrost

table-notes.note: (3) Does not apply to below-grade floors over heated crawl spaces.
(4) Unheated floors shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in Table 9.36.2.8.-C under their full bottom surface including exterior edges.
(5) Typically applies to floors-on-ground in full-height basements.
(6) Refers to undisturbed frost line before house is constructed.
(7) See for requirement on placement of insulation. The design of slabs-on-grade with an integral footing is addressed in Part 4 (see ).

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C and note in Article 9.36.2.8.(2)

sentence.text: Where an entire floor assembly falls into two of the categories listed in , the more stringent value shall apply. (See .)

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C in Article 9.36.2.8.(3)

sentence.text: Where the top of a section of foundation wall is on average less than 600 mm above the adjoining ground level, the above-ground portion of that section of wall shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in .

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C in Article 9.36.2.8.(5)

sentence.text: Except as provided in , floors-on-ground with embedded heating ducts, cables or pipes shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in under their full bottom surface including the edges.

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C in Article 9.36.2.8.(6)

sentence.text: Where only a portion of a floor-on-ground has embedded heating ducts, cables or pipes, that heated portion shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in under its full bottom surface to 1.2 m beyond its perimeter including exterior edges if applicable.

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C in Article 9.36.2.8.(7)

sentence.text: In addition to the requirements stated in , heated floors-on-ground shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in vertically

clause.text: around their perimeter, or
on the outside of the foundation wall, extending down to the level of the bottom of the floor.

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C in Article 9.36.2.8.(8)

sentence.text: Floors on permafrost shall be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in under the entire slab and around all edges, and under the integral perimeter footing.

amendment.description: Add reference to Table 9.36.2.8.-C in Article 9.36.2.8.(9)(a)

sentence.text: Slabs-on-grade with an integral perimeter footing shall

clause.text: be insulated to the effective thermal resistance required in under the entire slab and around all edges, but not under the integral perimeter footing, and
be constructed with skirt insulation having the same effective thermal resistance as the insulation installed under the slab.

amendment.description: Change standard from CAN/ULC-S742 to ASTM E2357 and remove Type A4 designation in Article 9.37.2.9.(1)(c)

new-content.text: a continuous air barrier system in accordance with and and a building assembly having an air leakage rate not greater than 0.20 L/(s·m2) when tested in accordance with where

amendment.description: Remove introductory sentence about airtight construction scope in Article 9.37.2.10.(7)
Change plumbing code reference from NPC to Book II in Article 9.36.4.3.(2)

new-content.text: Service water heating systems that use solar thermal technology shall be installed in accordance with the Book II (Plumbing Systems) of this Code.

amendment.description: Add missing subsection reference and exclude specific tables in Article 9.37.5.2.(1)

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "reference house" shall mean a hypothetical replica of the proposed house design using the same energy sources for the same functions and having the same environmental requirements, occupancy, climatic data and operating schedules, but made to comply with all applicable prescriptive requirements of , not including

amendment.description: Replace entire Article 9.37.5.3 with BC-specific compliance requirements and add note reference

note.text: (See .)

sentence.text: The performance compliance calculations shall determine the annual energy consumption of the proposed house and the house energy target of a reference house in accordance with

clause.text: this Subsection, or
the EnerGuide Rating System, version 15, and .

sentence.text: (See .)

Except as permitted by , the annual energy consumption of the proposed house in comparison to the house energy target of the reference house shall demonstrate a percent improvement in accordance with Table 9.37.6.3.-G. (See .)

Except for Tables 9.37.2.6.-C, 9.37.2.7.-D, and 9.37.2.8.-C, in establishing the house energy target, building components, systems and assemblies shall be accounted for in accordance with the prescriptive requirements of Subsections 9.37.2. to 9.37.4. for the climate zone under consideration.

In establishing the annual energy consumption, building components, systems and assemblies that are addressed in the scope of the prescriptive requirements of Subsections 9.37.2. to 9.37.4. shall be accounted for for the climate zone under consideration.

Where the construction techniques or building components, systems or assemblies used are more energy-efficient than those prescribed by the prescriptive requirements, the performance compliance calculations are permitted to take this increased performance level into account in the determination of the annual energy consumption, provided it can be quantified and is not dependent on occupant interaction.
Both the proposed and reference houses shall be modeled using the same climatic data, soil conditions, operating schedules in and temperature set-points.
For log homes, the annual energy consumption of the proposed house shall not exceed the house energy target of the reference house. (See .)

amendment.description: Add note reference to Subsection 9.37.2 in Article 9.37.5.6.(2)

sentence.text: The following building envelope assemblies and components shall be addressed in the energy model calculations:

clause.text: above-ground walls and roof-ceiling assemblies,
floors and walls in contact with the ground, and
doors, windows and skylights.

sentence.text: (See .)

amendment.description: Correct clause lettering from (e),(d) to (d),(e) and update reference to 9.37.7.3.(1) in Article 9.37.5.14.(2)

sentence.text: The energy model calculations for the reference house shall use the following values:

clause.text: 0.060 MJ/(m2×°C) for thermal mass,
a solar absorptance of 0.4 for the exterior walls, roofs and exposed floors,
0.26 for the solar heat gain coefficient of fenestration,
an airtightness of

subclause.text: 3.0 air changes per hour at 50 Pa pressure differential for attached zones, where the airtightness used for the proposed house is determined in accordance with using the unguarded method, and
2.5 air changes per hour at 50 Pa pressure differential otherwise, and

clause.text: the pressure exponent used for the proposed house where this value is less than 0.67, otherwise, 0.67.

amendment.description: Update reference from Sentence 9.37.6.3.(1) to Sentence 9.37.7.3.(1) in Article 9.37.5.10.(9)(c)
Update reference from Sentence 9.37.2.8.(4) to Sentence 9.37.2.8.(2) in Article 9.37.5.14.(9)
Change 'one or two' to '1 or 2' dwelling units in Article 9.37.5.14.(10)(a)
Change 'more than two' to 'more than 2' dwelling units in Article 9.37.5.14.(10)(b)
Insert new Subsection 9.36.6 Energy Step Code with Article 9.36.6.1 Application

sentence.text: Where a building contains more than one dwelling unit, the requirements of this Subsection shall apply to the energy performance of the building and not to individual dwelling units.

This Subsection is concerned with determining compliance with one of the energy performance Steps through modeling the energy performance of components, systems and assemblies that are installed in buildings and houses with or without a secondary suite, described in .

note.text: (See Note A-9.36.6.2.)

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "mechanical energy use intensity" shall mean a metric of the energy used over a year by the building, estimated by using an energy model in accordance with , normalized per square metre of floor area of conditioned space and expressed in kWh/(m2·year), for all of the following combined:

clause.text: space-heating equipment,
space-cooling equipment,
fans,
service water heating equipment,
pumps, and
auxiliary HVAC equipment (see Note A-9.36.6.2.(1)(f)).

sentence.text: Notwithstanding , for the purpose of this Subsection, the term "house" shall mean all houses, with or without a secondary suite, that

clause.text: have HVAC systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces,
have service water heating systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces, and
except for common spaces in the house with the secondary suite, do not share common spaces intended for occupancy with other dwelling units or houses.

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "thermal energy demand intensity" shall mean a metric of the annual heating required by the building for space conditioning and for conditioning of ventilation air, estimated by using an energy model in accordance with , normalized per square metre of floor area of conditioned space and expressed in kWh/(m2·year), taking into account all of the following:

clause.text: thermal transmittance of above-ground walls and roof-ceiling assemblies,
thermal transmittance of floors and walls in contact with the ground, or with space that is not conditioned space,
thermal transmittance and solar heat gain of windows, doors and skylights,
air leakage through the air barrier system,
internal heat gains from occupants and equipment, and
heat recovery from exhaust ventilation.

sentence.text: For the purpose of this Subsection, the term "Step" shall mean a Step referred to in Tables 9.36.6.3.-A to 9.36.6.3.-H.
Buildings conforming to the requirements of any of Steps 3 to 5 shall be designed and constructed to conform to the applicable energy performance requirements in

clause.text: Table 9.36.6.3.-G, or
a combination thereof.

table.title: Requirements for Buildings Located Where the Degree-Days Below 18°C Value is less than 3000(1)

row.entry: Performance Requirement of Building Equipment and Systems
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 30 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 20 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 15 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.

table.title: Requirements for Buildings Located Where the Degree-Days Below 18°C Value is 3000 to 3999(1)

row.entry: Performance Requirement of Building Equipment and Systems
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 40 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 30 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 20 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.

table.title: Requirements for Buildings Located Where the Degree-Days Below 18°C Value is 4000 to 4999(1)

row.entry: Performance Requirement of Building Equipment and Systems
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 50 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 40 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 25 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.

table.title: Requirements for Buildings Located Where the Degree-Days Below 18°C Value is 5000 to 5999(1)

row.entry: Performance Requirement of Building Equipment and Systems
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 70 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 55 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 35 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.

table.title: Requirements for Buildings Located Where the Degree-Days Below 18°C Value is 6000 to 6999(1)

row.entry: Performance Requirement of Building Equipment and Systems
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 90 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 70 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 50 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.

table.title: Requirements for Buildings Located Where the Degree-Days Below 18°C Value is greater than 6999(1)

row.entry: Performance Requirement of Building Equipment and Systems
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 105 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 80 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)
The applicable mechanical energy use intensity requirements in Table 9.36.6.3.-H
Thermal energy demand intensity ≤ 60 kWh/(m²·year), or thermal energy demand intensity not exceeding the value calculated in accordance with Sentence (4)

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.

table.title: Energy Performance Steps for Buildings and Houses(1)

row.entry: Total Volume of Conditioned Space Within the Building or House
> 300 m³ and where volume is not determined

note.text: (1) When a "house" is a dwelling unit forming part of a multi-unit building, as per Sentence 9.36.6.1.(1), requirements of Subsection 9.36.6. apply to entire building.
(2) See Table 9.36.7.4.
(3) See Sentence 9.36.6.5.(6)
(4) See Sentence 9.36.6.5.(7)

row.entry: Heating Degree-Days of Building Location(1), in Celsius Degree-Days
Amount of the Building's Conditioned Space Served by Space-Cooling Equipment

note.text: (1) See Sentence 1.1.3.1.(1) and Table C-2 in Appendix C.

sentence.text: Except as permitted by ,

clause.text: energy performance shall be calculated in conformance with and , and
airtightness shall be tested in accordance with .

sentence.text: Buildings designed and constructed to conform to Step 5 of any of the Tables referred to in and to the Passive House Planning Package, version 9 or newer, are deemed to comply with this Subsection if the energy model according to which the building is designed and constructed is prepared by a Certified Passive House Designer, or Certified Passive House Consultant, who is approved by the Passive House Institute.
For buildings conforming to the requirements of any of Steps 3 to 5, thermal energy demand intensity requirements may be calculated using the applicable following formula
for buildings located where degree-days below 18°C value is less than 3000, TEDIadjusted = TEDIstep + (TEDIhigher - TEDIstep)(HDDactual - HDDlowest)/500
for buildings located where degree-days below 18°C value is 3000 to 6999, TEDIadjusted = TEDIstep + (TEDIhigher - TEDIstep)(HDDactual - HDDlowest)/1000
for buildings located where degree-days below 18°C value is 7000 or greater, TEDIadjusted = TEDIstep + (TEDIhigher - TEDIstep)(HDDactual - HDDlowest)/1000

The energy performance of buildings and houses, when calculated in accordance with , shall conform to the target energy performance based on the total volume of conditioned space within the building or house for the energy performance metrics indicated in Table 9.36.6.3.-G such that

clause.text: the target "percent heat loss reduction" is met or exceeded, and
the target "percent improvement" is met or exceeded. (See Note A-9.36.6.3.(5)(b).)

sentence.text: Energy modeling shall be performed using a computer program that employs calculation methods that have been tested in accordance with , "Evaluation of Building Energy Analysis Computer Programs" with variations in the computer program from the range recommended therein reported in accordance with Division C.
Energy modeling shall conform to

clause.text: , or
Clauses 10.2.3.4.(1)(a) and (b) and Sentences 10.2.3.4.(3) and (4). (See Note A-9.36.6.4.(2)(b).)

sentence.text: For buildings conforming to the requirements of any of Steps 3 to 5, energy modeling shall account for the air leakage rate derived in accordance with . (See Note A-9.36.6.4.(3).)
Except where otherwise stated in this Article, the proposed and reference houses shall be modeled in accordance with to determine

clause.text: the annual energy consumption of the proposed house and the house energy target of the reference house, and
the annual gross space heat loss of the proposed and reference houses calculated in accordance with .

sentence.text: Reserved.
Where space heating is provided by a heat pump in the proposed house, the reference house shall be modelled using

clause.text: equipment of the same type as the secondary or back-up system in the proposed house, but made to comply with the energy efficiency requirements of , or
electric resistance heaters, where no back-up is provided in the proposed house.

sentence.text: Reserved.
The annual gross space heat loss shall be calculated as the sum of the cumulative heat loss from

clause.text: conduction across opaque and transparent elements of the building envelope,
air infiltration and exfiltration, and
mechanical ventilation.

sentence.text: The percent heat loss reduction shall be calculated by subtracting the annual gross space heat loss of the proposed house from the annual gross space heat loss of the reference house and dividing the result by the annual gross space heat loss of the reference house.

The percent improvement shall be calculated by subtracting the annual energy consumption of the proposed house from the house energy target of the reference house and dividing the result by the house energy target of the reference house.
Reserved.
The airtightness value used in the energy model for the proposed house shall be a design airtightness, until the airtightness has been measured in accordance with .

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.36.7.1.(1) - remove clause (b), add 9.37.6 reference

sentence.text: This Subsection is concerned with

clause.text: determining the airtightness of buildings and dwelling units and parts thereof

subclause.text: for use in the energy model calculations described in and , and
for use in determining the Airtightness Level.

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.37.7.2.(1)(a) with reserved - remove zone definition

clause.text: reserved,

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.37.7.2.(1)(b) with reserved - remove attached zone definition

clause.text: reserved,

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.37.7.3 - simplify to single sentence, remove clauses and sentences 2-3

sentence.text: Where airtightness is to be used as input to the energy model calculations, it shall be determined through a multi-point depressurization test carried out in accordance with , "Determination of the airtightness of building envelopes by the fan depressurization method."

amendment.description: Replace Article 9.37.7.4 - simplify to single sentence and table with 3 levels

sentence.text: Compliance with an Airtightness Level listed in shall be determined in accordance with this Article using the value of ACH50, NLA10, or NLR50 determined in accordance with .

amendment.description: Remove Subsection 9.37.7 - Tiered Energy Performance Compliance: Performance Path
Remove Subsection 9.37.8 - Tiered Energy Performance Compliance: Prescriptive Path
Insert new Section - Greenhouse Gas Emissions

sentence.text: Notwithstanding , the requirements of this Section apply only to buildings that

clause.text: are a house with or without a secondary suite including their common spaces,
contain only houses and common spaces whose total floor area does not exceed 20% of the total floor area of the building, and
are designed to one of the following

subclause.text: Step 3, 4 or 5 in Tables 9.36.6.3.-A to 9.36.6.3.-H, or

sentence.text: Where a building contains more than one house, with or without a secondary suite, the requirements of this Subsection shall apply to the greenhouse gas emissions of the building and not to the individual house. (See Note A-9.36.6.2.(2).)
Notwithstanding , for the purposes of this Section, the term "house" shall mean all houses, with or without a secondary suite, that

clause.text: have HVAC systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces,
have service water heating systems that serve only the house, only the secondary suite, or both the house and the secondary suite including their common spaces, and
except for common spaces in the house with the secondary suite, do not share common spaces intended for occupancy with other dwelling units or houses.

sentence.text: For the purposes of this Section, the term "greenhouse gas", expressed with the abbreviation "GHG", shall have the same meaning as defined in the Climate Change Accountability Act.

For the purposes of this Section, the term "greenhouse gas intensity", expressed with the abbreviation "GHGI", shall mean the total greenhouse gas emissions, expressed as kgCO2e per square metre of floor area of conditioned space per year, associated with the energy used by the building's systems.

For the purposes of this Section, the symbol "CO2e" shall mean carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide equivalent, with carbon dioxide equivalent meaning one or more greenhouse gases with a global warming potential equivalent to carbon dioxide.
For the purposes of this Section, the term "Emissions Level" shall mean an Emissions Level referred to in Table 9.37.1.3.
For the purposes of this Section, the term "total greenhouse gases" shall mean the total greenhouse gas emissions, expressed as kgCO2e/year, associated with the energy used by the building's systems.

For the purposes of this Section, the term "measure only" shall mean that the GHGI and the total greenhouse gases are calculated, as applicable, and the values reported in accordance with Subsection 2.2.8. of Division C.
Buildings conforming to the requirements of any of GHG Emission Levels EL-1 to EL-4 shall be designed and constructed to conform to one of the GHG emission compliance options in Table 9.37.1.3.

row.entry: Maximum GHG Emissions by House, Expressed in kg CO2e/year
Reduction of GHG Emissions by Energy Source of Building Systems
Maximum GHGI of the House, Expressed in kgCO2e/m2/year
Maximum GHG Emissions by House, Expressed in kgCO2e/year
Energy sources supplying heating systems have an emissions factor ≤ 0.011 kgCO2e/kWh
Energy sources supplying heating and service water heating systems have an emissions factor ≤ 0.011 kgCO2e/kWh
Energy sources supplying all building systems, including equipment and appliances, have an emissions factor ≤ 0.011 kgCO2e/kWh

note.text: Compliance for this option is demonstrated by meeting both GHGI and GHG emission requirements for each house.

Redundant or back-up equipment for systems and equipment listed in Sentence 9.36.5.4.(1). is permitted to be excluded, provided it is equipped with controls and is not required to meet to space-conditioning load of house.

sentence.text: The emissions factors associated with the use of energy utilities consumed by the building's systems shall be

clause.text: 0.011 kg CO2e/kWh for electricity,
0.180 kg CO2e/kWh for natural gas,
0.2155 kg CO2e/kWh for propane,
0.2579 kg CO2e/kWh for light fuel oil (standard),
0.2689 kg CO2e/kWh for heavy fuel oil,
0.2581 kg CO2e/kWh for diesel fuel (standard), and
0.0786 kg CO2e/kWh for wood fuel.

sentence.text: Calculations for greenhouse gas emissions compliance shall include:

clause.text: the energy sources for building systems described in Sentence 9.36.6.2.(1) using the emissions factors established in Sentence 9.37.1.3.(2), and
modeled in accordance with Article 9.36.6.4.

amendment.description: Delete NBC Subsection 9.36.7 Tiered Energy Performance O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.7.3 Tiered Energy Performance Compliance Calculations O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.2 Application O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.5 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.6 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.7 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.8 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.9 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.10 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
Delete NBC Article 9.36.8.11 O&FS entries from Table 9.38.1.1
